name: W3 Pull Morning Flow
description: 15 min — back/lat primer, core mini-block to get warm, then a flowing hip sequence to finish. Moving before stretching = deeper stretches. Core won't interfere with pull day — different muscle groups.

exercises.notes: Decompress the spine. Relax shoulders, let gravity pull you. Wakes up grip and lats for later.
From the hang: pull shoulder blades down and together without bending arms. Activates the pull muscles you'll need at the gym.
On hands and knees — reach one arm under, temple to floor, then open to ceiling. Flow back and forth. Opens thoracic spine and lats. Perfect pull-day prep.
Opposite arm/leg. Lower back GLUED to floor. Slow and controlled — 2 seconds out, 2 seconds back.
Arms by ears, legs hovering. Lower back pressed down. If 20s is easy, go 30s. If impossible, bend knees.
20s each side. Obliques + shoulder stability. Stack or stagger feet. Don't let hips sag.
Slow — touch elbow to opposite knee, fully extend other leg. No rushing. Quality over speed.
You're warm now — these will feel way better. Slow transitions, inhale arch, exhale round.
Flow: child's pose → slide forward through cobra → push back to child's pose. Smooth, continuous.
Flow: plank → push hips high to down dog → pedal feet → float back to plank. One continuous movement.
30s each side. From down dog, step one foot outside same hand. Sink hips. You're warm — go deeper than you think.
Sit, legs wide. Walk hands forward slowly. Then reach to each side. Your adductors are ready for this now.
Soles together, knees out. Fold forward. From the straddle, just bring your feet in — natural transition.
Roll onto back, grab feet, knees toward armpits. Rock gently side to side. This is your cooldown.

tips: 15 min — three parts: back primer (3 min), core work (5 min), yoga hip flow (7 min)
Order matters: primer → core gets you warm → stretches go deeper because you're not cold.
Part 3 is meant to FLOW. Don't reset between moves — transition from one into the next.
Cat-cow → child's-to-cobra → plank-to-down-dog → lizard is one continuous sequence on the floor.
Then sit up for straddle → butterfly → roll back for happy baby. The whole thing is one arc.
No pull-up bar at home? Do the hang/scap work at the gym as your first warmup set.
Short on time? Do Part 1 + Part 3, skip core. Still a solid 10 min flow.
